'''Dave Highton''' (born 31 January 1980) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]], [[Salford City Reds]] and the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League (Europe)|European Super League]]. He signed for [[Halifax RLFC|Halifax]] to play in [[2006 Rugby League National Leagues#National League One|National League One]], but injury forced him to retire aged just 25.<ref name="Injury forces Highton to retire">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/4573072.stm|title=Injury forces Highton to retire|publisher=BBC|date=1 January 2006|access-date=5 March 2021}}</ref><ref name="Dave Highton announces retirement">{{cite web|url=https://leagueunlimited.com/news/dave-highton-announces-retirement|title=Dave Highton announces retirement|publisher=League Unlimited|date=|accessdate=5 March 2021}}</ref>

Whilst at Warrington he was given a 10 month ban for steroid use<ref name="Highton accepts drugs ban">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1375712.stm|title=(David) Highton accepts drugs ban|publisher=BBC|date=7 June 2001|access-date=5 March 2021}}</ref> for which he was sacked by the [[Warrington Wolves|Wolves]].<ref name="Drug-ban Highton is sacked">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/3007312/Drug-ban-Highton-is-sacked.html|title=Drug-ban Highton is sacked|publisher=Daily Telegraph|date=14 June 2001|accessdate=5 March 2021}}</ref> He was later given a chance to resurrect his career at [[Salford City Reds|Salford]].<ref name="RL: Reds give Highton lifeline">{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/other-sport/rl-reds-give-highton-lifeline-1192623|title=RL: Reds give Highton lifeline|date=17 February 2007 |publisher=Manchester Evening News|accessdate=5 March 2021}}</ref>

His elder brothers [[Paul Highton]] and [[Chris Highton]] also played professional rugby league, with Paul playing for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at international level and Chris featuring for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] and the [[Swinton Lions]].<ref name="Chris Highton Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/chris-highton/summary.html|title=Chris Highton Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=|accessdate=5 March 2021}}</ref><ref name="Paul Highton Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/paul-highton/summary.html|title=Paul Highton Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=|accessdate=5 March 2021}}</ref>
